title: External navigation safety

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for External navigation safety; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to External navigation safety
sources.claim: External links should disclose meaningful destination behavior and apply deliberate new-context and referrer policies where required.

sources.locator: MDN HTML Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to External navigation safety
sources.claim: External links should disclose meaningful destination behavior and apply deliberate new-context and referrer policies where required.

canonicalExplanation.summary: External links should disclose meaningful destination behavior and apply deliberate new-context and referrer policies where required.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A link identifies that documentation opens on an external site and avoids forcing a new context unless the product requires it. Your goal is to configure an external or downloadable link without opener or expectation surprises. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Sections and landmarks; pf tools files; Meaningful links. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: External links should disclose meaningful destination behavior and apply deliberate new-context and referrer policies where required. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Opening a new tab is not always helpful, browser behavior evolves, and rel tokens do not replace URL trust or user communication. This draft therefore rejects: Every external link must open a new tab because that is always safer and more accessible.

includes: Use download, target, rel and referrer-related behavior only when justified and disclosed.
Configure an external or downloadable link without opener or expectation surprises.
excludes: Opening a new tab is not always helpful, browser behavior evolves, and rel tokens do not replace URL trust or user communication.

outcomes: Configure an external or downloadable link without opener or expectation surprises.

examples.title: External navigation safety: concrete case
examples.situation: A link identifies that documentation opens on an external site and avoids forcing a new context unless the product requires it.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A link identifies that documentation opens on an external site and avoids forcing a new context unless the product requires it.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Do not force a new browsing context by default; when one is required, disclose it and apply the appropriate rel policy.
examples.result: The result follows because Do not force a new browsing context by default; when one is required, disclose it and apply the appropriate rel policy.

examples.title: External navigation safety: boundary case
examples.situation: Opening a new tab is not always helpful, browser behavior evolves, and rel tokens do not replace URL trust or user communication.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Opening a new tab is not always helpful, browser behavior evolves, and rel tokens do not replace URL trust or user communication.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Every external link must open a new tab because that is always safer and more accessible.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Do not force a new browsing context by default; when one is required, disclose it and apply the appropriate rel policy.

recap.summary: You should now be able to configure an external or downloadable link without opener or expectation surprises. Keep this boundary in view: Opening a new tab is not always helpful, browser behavior evolves, and rel tokens do not replace URL trust or user communication.
